markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Unified-work-surface editor iteration 2, per operator notes (2026-06-12): (1) CKC embed views - inline NodeViews rendering the typed hsLink/embed nodes built in MT-163: insert picture [[HS_images]], embed slideshow [[HS_slideshow]], embedded albums, embedded videos - resolving REAL backend assets fail-closed (unresolvable embed renders a typed, visible error state; never mock data; never blank). (2) save-to-format export: HTML is the PRIMARY file projection (operator decision DEC-003) with data-hs-* attributes carrying typed link/embed semantics for near-lossless round-trip; TWO export modes: self-contained (assets base64-inlined - operator-confirmed) and reference-linked; plus markdown extractor (deliberately lossy, interop), plain text, and ProseMirror JSON. Authority remains the Postgres rich document; an export file is never authority. (3) Document-wide find/replace across prose AND embedded Monaco code blocks (case/whole-word/regex), VS Code-class.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Frontend-only lane: pnpm/vitest/Playwright; NO cargo builds; no src/backend changes (report any backend gap instead of mocking around it).
Bundled/lockfile-governed deps only (a markdown serializer such as prosemirror-markdown is acceptable); no CDN, no external apps.
Stable selectors + visual-debug surface for every new view; offline Playwright proof for embeds and export round-trip.
HTML import path (if implemented here) must sanitize: strip scripts/event handlers, fail-closed.
scope.expected_behavior: An operator (or model) inserts pictures, slideshows, albums, and videos inline and sees them render from real assets; exports the document to self-contained or reference-linked HTML (plus md/txt/json) via a save-to-format UI; and runs document-wide find/replace spanning prose and code blocks.
acceptance_criteria: Embed NodeViews render real resolvable assets; unresolvable -> typed visible error state (tested), never blank or mocked.
HTML export: data-hs-* semantics round-trip (export -> import -> identical doc structure proven in test); both modes work; base64 self-contained file opens standalone in a browser (Playwright proof).
Markdown/text/JSON exports produced; markdown explicitly documented as lossy.
Find/replace: case/whole-word/regex across prose + code blocks with match count + replace-all (tested).
Full app vitest suite + offline Playwright lane green; tsc + eslint clean.
verification: pnpm vitest run (full app suite) green; pnpm tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright: embed render + HTML export round-trip + standalone-open proof, zero external network requests.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests with counts, Playwright results.
Report any backend asset surface found missing (album/slideshow data source) as a precise gap - do not mock around it.
List stable selectors added for the visual-debug suite.

commits.slice: offline Playwright proofs (embeds render, find/replace E2E, standalone export)
adversarial hardening (sequence fan-out cap, pre-fetch inline size guard)

deliverables.embed_views: hsLink NodeView (app/src/components/HsLinkView.tsx + app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts): images/video/album/slideshow kinds resolve REAL workspace assets via GET /workspaces/:ws/assets/:id (+/content); img/video elements load real bytes; album/slideshow render sequenced viewers (prev/next + position) over ordered asset-id lists; non-media kinds keep the exact MT-163 chip. Fail-closed typed error states (role=alert, data-testid=hs-embed-error, data-error-kind in {no_workspace, empty_ref, absolute_path_rejected, traversal_rejected, scheme_rejected, invalid_ref, not_found, forbidden, server_error, network_error, kind_mismatch, media_load_failed}) - never blank, never mocked. Embed context flows RichDocumentView(workspace_id) -> RichTextEditor prop -> hsLink extension options; document model unchanged.
deliverables.save_to_format: app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts + export menu in RichTextEditor toolbar/palette. HTML primary projection (DEC-003) with data-hs-* typed semantics; export->import round-trip proven IDENTICAL doc structure incl. Monaco code block language/code/contentHash (monaco parse-rule priority fix). Modes: self_contained (real asset bytes base64-inlined; guards: videos NEVER inlined [documented size-blowup decision, visible data-hs-inline-skipped=video_size_guard notice, preload=none so a standalone file makes zero requests on open], 8MiB/image + 64MiB/doc caps with pre-fetch metadata guard) and reference_linked (backend asset content URLs). Markdown = deliberately lossy extractor (documented drops); plain text via renderText serializers; ProseMirror JSON envelope (schema_version + projection-only authority note). Import path sanitizes fail-closed (scripts/iframes/objects/forms removed, on* handlers stripped, javascript:/vbscript:/data:text-html/data:image-svg URLs removed, tab/control/U+FFFD scheme obfuscation normalized). Quiet blob download (no foreground window); outcome surfaced inline + machine-readable __HS_EDITOR_LAST_EXPORT__ global. Authority unchanged: exports are projections; save path untouched.
deliverables.find_replace: app/src/lib/editor/find_replace.ts + find_decorations.ts + code_block_find_registry.ts + FindReplacePanel.tsx. Document-wide scan across prose text AND monacoCodeBlock code attrs; case-sensitive/whole-word/regex toggles; live match count (n of m, truncation marker); next/prev wrap-around; ProseMirror decoration highlights (hs-find-match / --current; node-level hs-find-match-block for code blocks); in-Monaco range reveal+selection via NodeView registry (textarea fallback in degraded mode); replace one/all in ONE transaction (prose insertText + per-node setNodeMarkup with recomputed contentHash, descending-position order) = single undo step restoring the exact doc; selection preserved via mapping; typed invalid-regex error (role=alert); Mod-f/Mod-h keymap + toolbar Find button + palette commands.

vitest_full_suite.status: ALL PASS (fresh run incl. concurrent-lane MT-183/MT-158 code)

evidence.eslint: 0 findings in every MT-244 file; repo baseline of pre-existing errors in out-of-scope files (App.tsx, AtelierPanel, inference_lab, swarm, TiptapEditor, api.ts overload false-positive, third_party_notices.test) went 32 -> 31 errors vs HEAD baseline (identical failures verified pre-existing on HEAD)

names: classifies exactly the media kinds as embeds
matches mime families per embed kind
accepts UUID-shaped and opaque ids
rejects absolute Windows paths as absolute_path_rejected
rejects leading-slash and UNC paths as absolute_path_rejected
rejects traversal and separators as traversal_rejected
rejects schemes (http/javascript/file) as scheme_rejected
rejects empty and malformed ids
resolves real metadata to a typed content URL
fails closed with no_workspace when no workspace context is bound
rejects invalid refs before any network call
maps 404 -> not_found, 403 -> forbidden, 500 -> server_error
maps fetch rejection -> network_error and bad JSON -> server_error
fails closed on mime/kind mismatch (video asset in an images embed)
parses ordered comma-separated asset refs
resolves members independently - a broken member is a typed per-item error
treats an empty sequence as a typed empty_ref error
fails closed on an oversized sequence without fanning out requests (DoS guard)
renders a real from the typed backend asset content URL
renders a real <video controls> for a video embed
renders the sequenced slideshow viewer with prev/next over resolved members
renders a broken album member as a typed per-item error while the rest resolves
renders the typed visible error state for an unresolvable embed (never blank)
fails closed on a kind mismatch (video asset inside an images embed)
rejects an absolute-path ref client-side (backend MT-152 mirror)
fails closed with no_workspace when the editor has no embed context
keeps the exact MT-163 chip for non-media kinds (selectors unchanged)
round-trips export -> import to the identical document structure
round-trips the embedded Monaco code block byte-identically (hash included)
stamps data-hs-* typed semantics on links, embeds, code blocks, and the document
self-contained mode inlines REAL asset bytes as data: URLs
reference-linked mode emits the backend asset content URL
never inlines videos in self-contained mode (documented size guard)
skips the content fetch entirely when metadata declares an over-cap size (pre-fetch guard)
falls back to reference-linking for an image over the per-image cap
exports an unresolvable embed fail-closed with a typed visible marker
exports album/slideshow sequences as ordered real image lists
strips script elements, on* handlers, and executable URLs
imports a malicious export without executing or preserving script content
survives a code block whose code embeds
keeps structure: headings, marks, lists, tasks, quote, fence, wikilinks
is documented-lossy: the code-block round-trip hash does not survive
renders pipe tables
plain text keeps wikilink tokens and fenced code (renderText serializers)
JSON envelope round-trips and refuses non-doc payloads
slugs titles and never emits spaces
finds matches across prose AND code blocks in document order
honors case sensitivity
honors whole-word matching across prose and code
supports regex with capture groups
returns a typed error for an invalid regex (no throw)
guards zero-length regex matches (no infinite loop) and caps matches
rejects an over-long term with a typed error
expands $1..$9, $& and $$
replaces a prose match preserving the rest
replaces inside the code block and recomputes the round-trip hash
replace-all across prose + code is ONE undo step restoring the exact doc
expands regex groups during replace-all
keeps the selection in-document after replace-all (no selection loss)
indexAfterSelection wraps to the first match
selectMatch sets a text selection for prose and a node selection for code
opens via the toolbar button and counts prose + code matches
opens find via Mod-f and replace via Mod-h on the editor surface
navigates next/prev with wrap-around and highlights the current match
applies case/whole-word toggles and shows a typed error for invalid regex
replace-all rewrites prose AND code-block text; one undo restores all
replaces a single match leaving the others
closes on Escape and clears highlights
lists every export format and exports markdown end to end
exports reference-linked HTML through the palette command
resolves the find/replace chords to their special actions (MT-244)
mt244_tests.note: added to the existing MT-170 suite (7 tests total there)

playwright_offline_lane.command: cd app && pnpm run test:dependency-policy (playwright.dependency.config.ts, loopback-only + network kill-switch)
playwright_offline_lane.result: 6 passed (4 new MT-244 + 2 pre-existing), zero external requests in every test
mt244_specs: editor_embeds_export_find.spec.ts > embed NodeViews render real assets (img/video/slideshow) and fail closed on a broken ref [img naturalWidth=8 from pngjs-encoded PNG; <video> PLAYS a WebM recorded by Chromium itself via canvas captureStream+MediaRecorder (currentTime advances, videoWidth=64); slideshow 1/2 -> 2/2 navigation; typed not_found error visible]
editor_embeds_export_find.spec.ts > document-wide find/replace spans prose + Monaco code block in the real UI [4 matches (2 prose + 2 code), replace-all rewrites both, Monaco model length proof]

editor_embeds_export_find.spec.ts > self-contained HTML export downloads, inlines the image, and opens standalone from file:// with zero network [real download event, >=3 data:image/png;base64 srcs, video_size_guard notice, file:// open renders decoded img with ZERO network requests]
editor_embeds_export_find.spec.ts > HTML export round-trips through importHtml to the identical document (in-harness) [palette export path, data-hs-* semantics + workspace asset content URL]

gaps.gap: No backend album/slideshow LIST-SOURCE entity exists
gaps.detail: Searched src/backend/handshake_core: no album/collection table, no /albums route, no endpoint enumerating member assets for an album or slideshow id (only knowledge_document/embed.rs mentions albums as a typed-ref concept). Per the MT-244 contract the NodeView therefore resolves the CLOSEST REAL surface: the hsLink refValue carries an ordered comma-separated list of REAL asset ids ([[HS_slideshow:id1,id2]]), each resolved through GET /workspaces/:ws/assets/:id. Single repoint seam when an album entity lands: parseAssetRefList in app/src/lib/editor/embed_assets.ts.

gaps.gap: Asset content endpoint has no HTTP Range support
gaps.detail: GET /workspaces/:ws/assets/:id/content (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs get_asset_content) reads the full file and returns 200 without Accept-Ranges; browsers play small full-body videos but long-video seeking will need Range support backend-side. Frontend <video> works against the current surface; noting for the backend lane.

findings.risk: XSS via HTML export/import (script tags, on* handlers, javascript:/vbscript: URLs, svg data-URLs)
findings.fix: sanitizeImportedDom removes forbidden elements + on* attrs + executable URL schemes fail-closed BEFORE schema parse; all export assembly is DOM-API-only (serializer-escaped attrs/text, never string concat)
findings.tested: strips script elements/handlers/URLs; imports a malicious export without executing;

findings.risk: Scheme obfuscation defeating the URL filter (java\tscript:, NUL -> U+FFFD through HTML parsing)
findings.fix: normalizer strips whitespace, control chars (U+0000-U+001F) AND U+FFFD before scheme matching (real finding hit during test authoring: HTML parsers replace NUL with U+FFFD)

findings.risk: base64 size blowup inlining video into self-contained HTML
findings.fix: DOCUMENTED DECISION: videos are never inlined - reference-linked <video preload=none> + visible data-hs-inline-skipped=video_size_guard notice; images capped 8MiB each / 64MiB per doc; pre-fetch metadata size guard avoids pulling over-cap bytes into memory at all
findings.tested: vitest video guard + pre-fetch guard (content endpoint never hit) + Playwright standalone-open zero-network proof

findings.risk: Regex find DoS on huge docs (catastrophic backtracking, zero-length loops, unbounded match sets)
findings.fix: term length cap 1000, match cap 2000 with truncated flag surfaced in UI, 250ms scan time budget across segments, zero-length-match manual advance; RESIDUAL DOCUMENTED: one catastrophic exec on a single huge segment is uninterruptible in JS (same residual VS Code accepts)
findings.tested: zero-length guard, match cap truncation, over-long term typed error

findings.risk: Embed URL traversal / absolute path / scheme injection into asset URLs
findings.fix: validateAssetRef rejects drive letters, leading slash/UNC, path separators, .., schemes, oversize and non [A-Za-z0-9._-] ids CLIENT-SIDE before any request (mirror of backend MT-152 EmbedTarget rejection); asset ids are URL-encoded into typed endpoint URLs only

findings.risk: Sequence fan-out DoS ([[album:id1,...,id10000]] -> thousands of metadata requests)
findings.fix: MAX_SEQUENCE_ITEMS=100 cap, fail-closed typed invalid_ref error before ANY fetch
single transaction maps the selection head through tr.mapping and re-sets TextSelection.near(clamped) - caret stays in-document

findings.risk: Undo integrity after replace-all (mixed prose + code-attr rewrites)
findings.fix: ALL edits (prose insertText + per-node setNodeMarkup with recomputed contentHash) in ONE transaction applied in descending position order - exactly one undo step
findings.tested: undo restores byte-identical doc JSON incl. code hashes

findings.risk: Serialized Monaco block re-imported as plain codeBlock (silent loss of language/hash)
findings.fix: parse-rule priority 100 on monacoCodeBlock (outranks StarterKit generic pre rule) - found BY the round-trip test; also fixes the latent paste-path downgrade

findings.risk: Stale find highlights pointing at wrong text after edits
findings.fix: DecorationSet.map on doc changes between recomputes; panel rescans on every editor update; highlights cleared on close
findings.tested: closes on Escape and clears highlights

residual_risks_documented: Catastrophic regex backtracking within ONE huge segment is uninterruptible from JS (worker offload out of MT scope).
Self-contained exports embed reference-linked videos that require the backend running for playback (operator-visible notice in the file).

data:image/svg is stripped on IMPORT (svg can carry script); an svg workspace asset exported self-contained therefore loses its img src on re-import - the typed hsLink node itself round-trips losslessly.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
